Paper.title: Paper
Paper.description: Represents a research paper with its metadata, summaries, and access links.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Paper entity.

title.description: The full title of the research paper.

abstract.description: The original abstract or summary provided by the paper's source.

aiSummary.description: A concise, easy-to-understand summary of the paper generated by an AI model.

authors.description: A list of names of the authors who contributed to the paper.

publicationYear.description: The year when the paper was officially published.

citationCount.description: The number of times this paper has been cited by other research.

externalUrl.description: A direct URL to the paper's page on a publisher's site, DOI resolver, or similar external resource.

pdfUrl.description: A direct URL to download the PDF version of the paper, if available.

domain.description: The primary academic domain or field to which the paper belongs (e.g., 'Computer Science', 'Physics', 'Biology').

isOpenAccess.description: Indicates whether the paper is freely accessible without subscription or payment.

retrievedAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the paper's metadata was last retrieved or updated from external APIs.

semanticScholarId.description: Unique identifier for the paper within the Semantic Scholar database.

arXivId.description: Unique identifier for the paper within the arXiv e-print archive.

doi.description: Digital Object Identifier (DOI) for the paper, providing a persistent link to its location on the internet.

UserProfile.title: UserProfile
UserProfile.description: Stores user-specific data and preferences for PaperBridge, linked to an external authentication system.

id.description: Unique identifier for the UserProfile entity, typically correlating with an external authentication user ID.

email.description: The user's email address, used for identification purposes (not for authentication system storage).

firstName.description: The first name of the user.

lastName.description: The last name of the user.

bookmarkedPaperIds.description: An array of IDs referencing Paper entities that the user has bookmarked. (Relationship: UserProfile N:N Paper)

lastLoginAt.description: Timestamp indicating the user's last successful login.

SearchHistory.title: SearchHistory
SearchHistory.description: Records details of a user's past search queries and applied filters.

id.description: Unique identifier for the SearchHistory entry.

userId.description: Reference to the UserProfile entity that performed this search. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N SearchHistory)

queryText.description: The actual text query entered by the user during the search.

searchTimestamp.description: Timestamp indicating when the search was performed.

appliedFilters.description: A string representation (e.g., JSON string) of any filters applied during the search (e.g., year range, domain).

resultCount.description: The number of search results returned for this query.

definition.description: Stores publicly accessible research paper metadata and AI-generated summaries. All documents in this collection are globally readable. No specific user authorization data is denormalized here as access is public.

params.name: paperId
params.description: Unique identifier for a specific research paper.

definition.description: Stores private user profile information, including bookmarked paper IDs. Access is restricted to the owner of the profile (authenticated user's UID must match userId). Ownership is established via the document path.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique ID of the authenticated user, corresponding to request.auth.uid.

definition.description: Stores individual search history entries for a specific user. Access is restricted to the owner of the history (authenticated user's UID must match userId). Includes denormalized 'userId' field matching the parent path for explicit ownership and authorization independence.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique ID of the authenticated user, corresponding to request.auth.uid and the parent UserProfile.

params.name: historyId
params.description: Unique identifier for a specific search history entry.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore structure for PaperBridge prioritizes secure, scalable, and debuggable authorization by adhering to the core design principles and mandates. 

**Authorization Independence:**
1.  **Public Data (`/papers/{paperId}`):** The `papers` collection is designed for public access. Since all papers are intended to be globally readable, there are no specific user-based authorization dependencies. This inherently ensures authorization independence as no parent document `get()` operations are ever required for access checks.
2.  **User-Owned Data (`/users/{userId}` and `/users/{userId}/searchHistory/{historyId}`):** For `UserProfile` and `SearchHistory`, authorization is entirely path-based. A user's profile is stored at `/users/{userId}` where `{userId}` is directly `request.auth.uid`. Similarly, a user's search history is stored in a subcollection at `/users/{userId}/searchHistory/{historyId}`. This design mandates that access rules for these documents will check `request.auth.uid == userId` (where `userId` is the wildcard from the path). This pattern ensures atomic operations and authorization independence because access decisions are made solely on the `request.auth.uid` and the document's own path, without needing to fetch any parent document data.
3.  **Denormalization for Ownership:** While the path-based authorization is primary, for `SearchHistory`, the `userId` field is also present within the document. This serves as an invariant, explicitly linking the history entry to its owner within the document itself, even though the path already guarantees this for security rules.

**QAPs (Rules are not Filters):**
1.  **Public Collections (`/papers`):** The `papers` collection is structured to have a homogeneous security posture: all documents are publicly readable. This allows for simple and performant security rules (e.g., `allow read: if true;`). A `list` operation on `/papers` will return all documents, which is the intended secure behavior (QAP).
2.  **User-Scoped Collections (`/users/{userId}` and `/users/{userId}/searchHistory`):** For user-specific data, `list` operations are implicitly secured by the path. A query to `/users/{request.auth.uid}/searchHistory` will only ever return search history entries belonging to the authenticated user. The security rules will enforce `request.auth.uid == userId` for all read operations, including `list`. This satisfies the QAP principle as the rules themselves act as a precise filter, ensuring users only retrieve their own data without relying on client-side filtering or inefficient database queries for security.

**Structural Segregation & Access Modeling:**
-   `papers` is a top-level collection due to its public, global nature.
-   `users/{userId}` establishes private, path-based ownership for `UserProfile` data, leveraging the authenticated user's UID.
-   `users/{userId}/searchHistory/{historyId}` continues this hierarchical path for user-owned `SearchHistory` entities, ensuring tight coupling to the user profile and simplifying rules.
